description.summary: Jörmungandr (the Midgard Serpent or World Serpent) is an enormous sea serpent in Norse mythology, so large it encircles the entire world (Midgard) and grasps its own tail.
description.full: Jörmungandr (the Midgard Serpent or World Serpent) is an enormous sea serpent in Norse mythology, so large it encircles the entire world (Midgard) and grasps its own tail. Son of Loki and the jötunn Angrboða, brother to Fenrir and Hel, Jörmungandr was thrown into the ocean by Odin as a child and grew to vast size. The serpent is the arch-enemy of **Thor**, the thunder god. They encountered each other twice before their final battle: during Thor's fishing trip (where Thor nearly caught the serpent), and at Útgarða-Loki (where the serpent was disguised as a cat). At **Ragnarök**, Jörmungandr will emerge from the ocean, poison the sky, and engage Thor in their destined mutual destruction—Thor will kill the serpent but die from its venom after taking nine steps. Jörmungandr represents cosmic order and boundaries, similar to the ouroboros symbol of eternal cycles.
